title: Consumer Goroutines, Cancellation, and Shutdown · Runtime Contract: Consumer Tag Generation
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must own ConsumeWithContext channels, cancellation, in-flight handlers, deadlines, and close order under one supervisor; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the consumer tag generation increment in a leak-resistant Go consumer lifecycle state machine. Learners pr…
objectives: Track consumer tags and channel generations so stale cancellation, delivery, and close events cannot mutate current state; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Report ready only after connection, topology, channel, prefetch, consume registration, and required dependencies are ready; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Consumer Tag Generation · Theory Consumer Tag Generation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 8033 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to sequence consumer readiness, cancellation, handler draining, deadline, and close; separate deterministic browser proof from race-enabled and live-broker evidence; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
